pattern: Remembering the next suffix to try for every base name, instead of always restarting the search at (1), keeps each folder creation fast even across a long run of repeats.
hints: Track, for every name that has been used (whether typed directly or produced as a suffixed name), that it is now taken.
When a requested name collides, don't always start the search at (1) - remember the next suffix you expect to need for that base name and resume from there next time.

The remembered suffix is only a starting guess: it might already be taken too (if that exact suffixed name was typed directly earlier), so keep incrementing until you find one that is free, then save both the new name and the updated next-guess for the base name.

edge_inputs.description: no collisions at all, every requested name passes through unchanged
a repeated request suffixed twice in a row
an explicit suffixed name is typed before the collision that would have produced it
a single request never needs a suffix
the remembered next-suffix guess collides with a directly-typed name and must keep searching
two independent base names must not interfere with each other's suffix counters
a name that already looks suffixed is itself just a plain string to dedupe

stress.description: near-max count drawn from a small pool, forcing long suffix chains
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    pool = [f"name{i}" for i in range(20)]
    return [[rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n)]]

stress.description: mostly-unique names with occasional repeats and pre-typed suffixes
